arts.ttl: “Different kettle of fish” – Brentford man on playing at ‘highest level’ with Bees and his peculiar ‘dream’
arts.sum: Sepp van den Berg has been a regular for Brentford since joining them from Liverpool on a permanent transfer in August 2024. Thomas Frank has mainly used him as a centre-back and has also deployed him on the right side of defence. Van den Berg made his Brentford debut when he came off the bench The post “Different kettle of fish” – Brentford man on playing at ‘highest level’ with Bees and his peculiar ‘dream’ appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Thomas Tuchel to add Brentford coach to England backroom staff ahead of World Cup qualifiers?
arts.sum: New England boss Thomas Tuchel is reportedly set to appoint Brentford coach Justin Cochrane to his backroom staff ahead of March's World Cup qualifiers.

arts.ttl: Chips in strips: Brentford move to stop counterfeit market in match-worn shirts
arts.sum: Microchips aim to prove authenticity of player-worn kit First use comes in Wednesday’s visit of Everton Brentford are to integrate microchips into their match-day shirts that will prove their authenticity, in a move against the growing market for counterfeit football kits. Beginning with Wednesday night’s Premier League fixture against Everton, Brentford will become the first English side to wear shirts that provide a digital guarantee of their provenance, crucial for merchandise often sold for substantial sums at auction. arts.ttl: Everton contact PGMOL over controversial Man Utd penalty decision
arts.sum: Everton boss David Moyes confirmed that the club had been in contact with PGMOL after they were denied a penalty against Manchester United on Saturday. The Toffees were initially awarded a spot kick on the field by referee Andy Madley after Ashley Young was seemingly fouled in the box in added time at Goodison Park.
